instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/life-insurance-guide/ulip/parameters-to-consider-before-buying-ulip.html
Parameters To Consider Before Buying ULIPs
Out of the many investment options available for long-term wealth creation, ULIPs occupy a special place in the roster of financial products. Ranging from multiple tax benefits to a life insurance cover, this investment option offers a plethora of benefits to the average investor. If you are wondering what a ULIP policy is, and what the factors that you should consider before investing in one are, here is some in depth information that can help you out. Read More
What is a ULIP?A Unit Linked Insurance Plan, also known as a ULIP, is an investment vehicle that combines life insurance and investment. By investing in a ULIP, you not only get to create long-term market linked wealth, but also receive a life cover that provides your family with financial protection in the event of your death.
A ULIP policy is one of the most versatile investment options available since it gives you complete freedom to choose and customize the plan according to your financial goals and objectives. Right from the amount of premium and the tenure to the type of fund and sum assured, you could customize a ULIP plan as per the policy terms and conditions to ensure that it is in tune with your life goals and requirements.
Types of ULIP plans in IndiaNow that you are aware of what a ULIP policy is, let us look at the different types of ULIPs that are available in India.
Goal-based ULIPsThese types of ULIP plans are geared towards helping you achieve your life goals and dreams, irrespective of what they may be. Whether your goal is to create long-term wealth, go on an extended vacation with your family, or take care of your child’s future, goal-based ULIPs are designed to help you achieve the same.
Retirement-focused ULIPsIf you are an investor who feels that planning for your retirement is the need of the hour and far more crucial, then retirement-focused ULIPs are the right option for you. The investment strategies of such a type of a ULIP investment is geared towards providing you with market linked returns after your retirement, thereby enabling you to live your life on your own terms.
Retirement-focused ULIPs are designed to offer you a steady stream of income by way of systematic partial withdrawals and usually come with whole life insurance covers as well.
Parameters to consider before investing in ULIP plansWith ULIPs, there is a plethora of options to choose from. While having these many options is advantageous to an investor, it can also get quite difficult when it comes to choosing the right plan. Here are some parameters that you should consider and keep in mind before investing in ULIPs –
Your goals and objectivesNothing is more crucial than ensuring that the investments that you make align with your goals and objectives in life. In order to do that, you should first be aware of what your goals are. Since your life goals and objectives are what define and shape the trajectory of your investments, always consider them before investing in a ULIP.
Only by putting enough thought into your goals will you be able to ascertain the quantum of investment required and the period within which you would want your goals to be accomplished. For instance, if your life goal is to purchase your own home in 10 years, choosing a ULIP that allows you to achieve that goal becomes easier.
Your risk toleranceNot all investments carry the same amount of risk. As far as ULIPs are concerned, this statement could not be truer. Since the funds in a ULIP carry varying levels of risk, knowing your risk appetite becomes essential. This way, you can ensure that your risk tolerance levels and the fund risk matches perfectly.
For instance, equity funds generally carry more risk, whereas debt funds are low risk. On a similar note, a combination of both equity and debt carries a more moderate level of risk. Therefore, knowing your risk tolerance allows you to choose the right kind of funds to invest in.
ULIP chargesBefore you go about investing in a ULIP, it is essential to first take a good, hard look at the various charges that are levied by the insurer. In addition to the standard fund management charges and policy administration charges, there are several other ULIP charges that the insurer levies. This includes premium allocation charges, mortality charges, rider charges, fund switching charges, and top-up charges, to name a few. Since ULIP charges can tend to eat into your final returns, always make sure to check them out thoroughly.
ULIP fund performanceAnother major factor that you should consider before choosing to invest in a ULIP is the fund’s performance and the ULIP NAV over the years. Irrespective of the type of funds that you choose, always make sure to check the performance of at least 5 years. Although past performance does not guarantee future performance, this exercise would give you a fair idea of how the fund and the ULIP NAV has performed during various points in time and under different market conditions.
Benefits of ULIPsAs you have already seen above, ULIPs come with a plethora of benefits for an investor. Here is a quick look at some of them –
Offers dual benefits of both life insurance and investments.Can be customized according to your needs and requirements.Offers complete freedom of choice with respect to the choice of funds.Enables you to switch between the various funds.Comes with a top-up facility that allows you to make additional investments.Avail Tax benefits under section 80 C while the maturity benefit or death benefit is tax free section 10 (10D) of the Income Tax Act,1961. ULIPs have also been kept exempt from LTCG tax. Tax benefits are subject to provisions mentioned therein.Makes use of rupee cost averaging to lower your cost of investmentConclusionConsidering the fact that you would be investing your hard-earned money in a ULIP, always make sure that you consider and account for all the possible factors before investing in one. This way, you can ensure that your financial goals and objectives are satisfied on time.
#Survey conducted by brand equity – Nielsen in March 2020
~Tax benefits as per prevailing Income tax laws shall apply. Please check with your tax consultant for eligibility.
**Past performance is not indicative of future performance.
The above information is for general understanding and is meant to educate the general public at large. The reader will have to verify the facts, law and content with the prevailing tax statutes and seek appropriate professional advice before acting on the basis of the above information.
IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajlifeinsurance.com) carefully before concluding a sale. Bajaj Life Insurance Limited (Formerly known as Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited) Reg. Office Address: Bajaj Insurance House, Airport Road, Yerawada, Pune - 411006. CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959,  call us on Customer Care No. 020-6712 1212 , mail us on: customercare@bajajlife.com. The Logo of Bajaj Life Insurance Limited is provided on the basis of license given by Bajaj Finserv Ltd. to use its “Bajaj” Logo.
---------------------------------------------------------
